expectations.text: Advises not to change things while progress is still happening
expectations.evidence: States: 'a program that works is better than a perfect program you haven't tested yet.'

expectations.text: At plateau: first determine whether more stress or less stress is needed
expectations.evidence: Hierarchy of changes starts with adherence/consistency audit before program changes.

expectations.text: Recommends small, targeted changes (volume, intensity, frequency, or exercise selection)
expectations.evidence: Lists: adherence, intensity, exercise selection, volume, frequency as ordered changes.

expectations.text: Evaluate each change for a few weeks before making another
expectations.evidence: States: 'Give it 3-4 weeks minimum' and 'change one variable at a time.'

expectations.text: If serial tweaks fail, then moving to a new program is reasonable
expectations.evidence: Lists red flags warranting a switch: 12+ weeks no progress, structural conflicts, persistent pain.
